paper.title: Origin and Status of Low-Mass Candidate Hypervelocity Stars

review.summary: Paper presents a chemodynamical analysis of six low-mass dwarf stars previously claimed as HVS candidates by Palladino et al. (2014). Contains 6 LaTeX tables: observation log, comparison star parameters, program star chemical abundances, proper motion comparisons, velocity/orbital parameters, and origin summary. Uses Gaia DR2 proper motions and moderate-resolution spectroscopy. All data products are self-contained in the paper's LaTeX tables; no external machine-readable data files are referenced. External resources are limited to infrastructure URLs (Gaia, SDSS, DSS, UKIDSS) cited in the text or acknowledgements.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Records the spectroscopic observation details (dates, exposure times, S/N) for the 6 program HVS candidates and 8 standard comparison stars.

columns.name: Object
columns.meaning: Star identifier (SDSS name for program stars, HIP/HR name for standard stars)

columns.name: Magnitude
columns.meaning: Reddening-corrected r-band magnitude (r_0) for program stars; V-band magnitude for standard stars

columns.name: Date (UT)
columns.meaning: UT date(s) of spectroscopic observation

columns.name: Exposure | Time (s)
columns.meaning: Individual exposure time(s) in seconds; multiple values separated by commas indicate multiple exposures

columns.name: N_\rmobs
columns.meaning: Number of individual spectroscopic observations co-added

columns.name: S/N
columns.meaning: Signal-to-noise ratio of the co-added spectrum after data reduction

columns.name: Pal14~ID
columns.meaning: Object identification number from Palladino et al. (2014) catalog

internal_tables.evidence: Table caption and tabnote describe all columns. Section 2 (Spectroscopic Observations and Reduction) provides additional context on the DIS instrument setup and observation strategy.
internal_tables.comments: Table is split into two sections: program stars (6 objects) and standard/comparison stars (8 objects). Standard stars were observed at multiple S/N levels to calibrate systematic offsets.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the adopted (literature) stellar parameters and chemical abundances for the 8 comparison stars used to validate the abundance analysis pipeline.

columns.name: [Cr/H]

internal_tables.evidence: Table caption, tabnote with reference key, and Section 3 text explaining that adopted values are averages from multiple literature sources.
internal_tables.comments: References column uses numeric codes decoded in the table note. When multiple references were available, an average was taken.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the final derived stellar parameters and chemical abundances for the 6 program stars after systematic offset correction — this is the core data product of the paper.

source_refs.caption: Derived stellar parameters and chemical abundances for our program stars \label{tab3}
source_refs.label: tab3

columns.name: SDSS~ID
columns.meaning: SDSS object identifier (J2000 coordinate-based name)

columns.name: Pal14~ID
columns.meaning: Object identification number from Palladino et al. (2014) HVS candidate catalog

columns.name: T_\rm eff (K)
columns.meaning: Effective temperature with uncertainty, corrected for systematic offset

columns.name: \log~g
columns.meaning: Surface gravity (logarithmic) with uncertainty, corrected for systematic offset

columns.name: [Fe/H]
columns.meaning: Iron abundance relative to solar with uncertainty, corrected for systematic offset

columns.name: [Mg/Fe]
columns.meaning: Magnesium-to-iron abundance ratio

columns.name: [Si/Fe]
columns.meaning: Silicon-to-iron abundance ratio

columns.name: [Ca/Fe]
columns.meaning: Calcium-to-iron abundance ratio

columns.name: [Ti/Fe]
columns.meaning: Titanium-to-iron abundance ratio

columns.name: [Cr/Fe]
columns.meaning: Chromium-to-iron abundance ratio

columns.name: [Ni/Fe]
columns.meaning: Nickel-to-iron abundance ratio

columns.name: [\alpha/Fe]
columns.meaning: Mean alpha-element abundance ratio, average of [Mg/Fe], [Si/Fe], [Ca/Fe], and [Ti/Fe]

internal_tables.evidence: Table caption and tabnote explicitly describe systematic offset corrections and typical abundance uncertainties. Section 3 (Estimation of Stellar Parameters and Chemical Abundances) fully documents the SP_Ace pipeline and correction procedure.
internal_tables.comments: Values reported with ± uncertainties. Systematic offsets were derived from comparison stars at S/N < 50. Typical errors: σ[Mg/Fe]=0.27, σ[Si/Fe]=0.17, σ[Ca/Fe]=0.15, σ[Ti/Fe]=0.18, σ[Cr/Fe]=0.20, σ[Ni/Fe]=0.18 dex.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Compares proper motions from three different sources (SDSS, Ziegerer+2015, Gaia DR2) for the 6 program stars, demonstrating why earlier HVS claims were incorrect.

source_refs.caption: Proper motions of our program stars from different catalogs \label{tab4}
source_refs.label: tab4

columns.name: SDSS~ID
columns.meaning: SDSS object identifier

columns.name: Pal14~ID
columns.meaning: Object identification number from Palladino et al. (2014)

columns.name: SDSS | \mu_\alphacos\delta | (mas~yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension from SDSS catalog (used by Palladino+2014), with uncertainty

columns.name: Ziegerer et al. (2015) | \mu_\delta | (mas~yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination from SDSS, with uncertainty

columns.name: \multicolumn2c\it Gaia~DR2 | \mu_\alphacos\delta | (mas~yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension from Ziegerer et al. (2015) re-measurement, with uncertainty

columns.name: \mu_\delta | (mas~yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination from Ziegerer et al. (2015), with uncertainty

columns.name: \mu_\alphacos\delta | (mas~yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension from Gaia DR2, with uncertainty

columns.name: \mu_\delta | (mas~yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination from Gaia DR2, with uncertainty

internal_tables.evidence: Table caption and Section 4.1 (Kinematic Properties) text explicitly compare the three proper motion sources and explain the discrepancies. The multi-column header structure clearly groups sources.
internal_tables.comments: The table uses multi-column headers to group measurements from SDSS, Ziegerer+2015, and Gaia DR2. Two stars (Pal14 IDs 7, 10) lack Ziegerer+2015 measurements. Gaia DR2 proper motions are significantly more precise and show the earlier SDSS-based values were inflated.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the derived space velocities (U,V,W), distances, orbital parameters, and Galactic rest-frame velocities for the 6 program stars — the key kinematic results.

source_refs.caption: Velocities and orbital parameters of our program stars \label{tab5}
source_refs.label: tab5

columns.name: SDSS~ID
columns.meaning: SDSS object identifier

columns.name: Pal14~ID
columns.meaning: Object identification number from Palladino et al. (2014)

columns.name: v_\rm r | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity measured from the observed spectra, with uncertainty

columns.name: d | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Photometric distance estimated by the SEGUE Stellar Parameter Pipeline (SSPP), with uncertainty

columns.name: U | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Space velocity component toward the Galactic anticenter (positive outward), with uncertainty

columns.name: V | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Space velocity component in the direction of Galactic rotation, with uncertainty

columns.name: W | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Space velocity component toward the North Galactic Pole, with uncertainty

columns.name: r_\rm min | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Minimum Galactocentric distance reached during the orbit, with uncertainty

columns.name: r_\rm max | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Maximum Galactocentric distance reached during the orbit, with uncertainty

columns.name: Z_\rm max | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Maximum distance from the Galactic plane reached during the orbit, with uncertainty

columns.name: e
columns.meaning: Orbital eccentricity, e = (r_max - r_min) / (r_max + r_min), with uncertainty

columns.name: V_\rm GRF | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Galactic rest-frame velocity, with uncertainty

internal_tables.evidence: Table tabnote explains LSR velocity and solar peculiar motion assumptions. Section 4.1 fully documents the MWPotential2014 gravitational potential, distance estimation method, Monte Carlo error propagation, and coordinate definitions.
internal_tables.comments: U, V, W computed assuming V_LSR=220 km/s and solar peculiar motion (-10.1, 4.0, 6.7) km/s. Orbital parameters derived under MWPotential2014 with R_sun=8.0 kpc. Uncertainties from 100-iteration Monte Carlo resampling. All six stars confirmed bound to the MW (V_GRF << V_esc=533 km/s).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summary table assigning each program star to a likely Galactic component (thin/thick disk, bulge) and proposing possible origin scenarios based on combined chemical and kinematic evidence.

source_refs.caption: Possible origin of our program stars \label{tab6}
source_refs.label: tab6

columns.name: SDSS~ID
columns.meaning: SDSS object identifier

columns.name: Pal14~ID
columns.meaning: Object identification number from Palladino et al. (2014)

columns.name: Galactic component
columns.meaning: Assigned Galactic stellar population based on combined chemical abundance patterns and kinematic membership probabilities (Bulge, Thick disk, or Thin disk)

columns.name: Possible origin(s)
columns.meaning: Proposed astrophysical origin: Accreted or heated (from disrupted dwarf galaxy or dynamical heating), Runaway (ejected from disk), Runaway or heated (ambiguous), or typical disk star (no exotic origin)

internal_tables.evidence: Table caption. Section 5 (Discussion) provides detailed object-by-object justification for each assignment. Section 6 (Summary and Conclusions) recapitulates the assignments and origin hypotheses.
internal_tables.comments: The Galactic component assignments combine kinematic probabilities (Bensby+2003 method) with chemical tagging (alpha-element and iron-peak abundance patterns vs. reference samples). Origin inferences are interpretive, not definitive. Two stars (Pal14 IDs 7, 17) are classified as typical disk stars with no exotic origin.

external_resources.description: SDSS SkyServer Navigate tool, cited as the image source used by Ziegerer et al. (2015) to re-measure proper motions for the Pal14 HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: motions for the 14 HVS candidates that Pal14 reported, using images from SDSS\footnote{\url{http://skyserver.sdss3.org/public/en/tools/chart/navi.aspx}},

external_resources.evidence: Introduction text describing Ziegerer+2015's proper motion re-measurement methodology, with this URL as a footnote for the SDSS imaging source.
external_resources.comments: This resource was used by Ziegerer et al. (2015), not directly by the present paper. The URL appears as a LaTeX footnote.

external_resources.description: Digitized Sky Survey (DSS) plate finder, cited as an image source used by Ziegerer et al. (2015) for proper motion measurements.
external_resources.evidence: Introduction text footnote for DSS as one of three imaging sources used by Ziegerer+2015.
external_resources.comments: This resource was used by Ziegerer et al. (2015), not directly by the present paper.

external_resources.description: UKIRT Infrared Deep Sky Survey (UKIDSS) website, cited as an image source used by Ziegerer et al. (2015) for proper motion measurements.
external_resources.evidence: Introduction text footnote for UKIDSS as one of three imaging sources used by Ziegerer+2015.
external_resources.comments: This resource was used by Ziegerer et al. (2015), not directly by the present paper. The accompanying citation is to Lawrence et al. (2007).

external_resources.description: ESA Gaia mission website. The paper uses Gaia Data Release 2 proper motions as the primary astrometric data source for kinematic analysis of the six program stars.

source_refs.context: Gaia}\footnote{\url{https://www.cosmos.esa.int/gaia}}, processed by the {\it Gaia} Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC)

external_resources.evidence: Acknowledgements section footnote. The main body of the paper (Sections 1, 4, 5) explicitly uses Gaia DR2 proper motions (Gaia Collaboration et al. 2018) for all kinematic calculations.
external_resources.comments: Gaia DR2 is the primary data source underpinning the paper's kinematic conclusions (Tables 4 and 5). The URL appears as an acknowledgement footnote.
